cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An eighteenth-century intellectual movement that applied reason and empirical inquiry to human society, arguing that natural laws governed human affairs and that rational reform could improve government, law, and society — ideas that fed directly into the Atlantic revolutions.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The idea, articulated by Enlightenment philosophers, that individuals possess inherent rights (life, liberty, property) that exist prior to and independent of any government — governments derive legitimacy from protecting these rights.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: John Locke's theory that government derives its authority from the consent of the governed — if a government fails to protect natural rights, citizens have the right to alter or abolish it.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A political ideology emphasizing individual liberty, constitutional government, rule of law, and protection of private property — the dominant ideology of the Atlantic revolutions.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The political ideology that a people sharing a common culture, language, or history should have their own sovereign state — a major force driving revolutions and unification movements from 1750 to 1900.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The period of radical political violence during the French Revolution (1793–1794) in which the Committee of Public Safety, led by Robespierre, executed approximately 17,000 people as enemies of the revolution.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The economic system in which private individuals own the means of production and compete in markets, with prices and production determined by supply and demand rather than government direction.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An economic and political ideology arguing that the means of production should be collectively owned and that the state should distribute resources according to social need rather than market prices — a response to industrial capitalism's inequalities.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels's theory of scientific socialism, arguing that history progresses through class struggle, that capitalism would be overthrown by the proletariat, and that a classless stateless society would emerge.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Adam Smith's doctrine that economies function best when government does not interfere — markets should be governed by the 'invisible hand' of supply and demand rather than state regulation or mercantile policy.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The shift from agrarian, home-based production (cottage industry) to factory-based manufacturing, powered first by water mills and then by steam engines — beginning in Britain in the late eighteenth century.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A second wave of industrialization beginning in the 1870s characterized by steel production, chemicals, electricity, and new industries in Germany and the United States — distinguished from the first Industrial Revolution by its technological character and broader geographic spread.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A system of production in which workers sell their labor for wages and work in centralized facilities using machinery owned by capitalists — the dominant form of industrial production that replaced cottage industry and craft production.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The British enclosure of common agricultural land into private fenced property — displaced peasant farmers from the land and created a mobile workforce available for factory employment.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The pre-industrial production system in which merchants distributed raw materials to rural households, who produced finished goods at home — later displaced by the factory system.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: James Watt's improved version (1769) became the central technology of the Industrial Revolution, powering factories, railways, and steamships by burning coal to produce mechanical force.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The urban industrial working class created by capitalism — those who own no means of production and must sell their labor for wages; Karl Marx's revolutionary class.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The property-owning middle class of capitalists, merchants, and professionals — the class that owned the means of production in Marx's analysis and whose interests the capitalist state served.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Organized associations of workers who negotiate collectively with employers over wages, hours, and working conditions — emerged as a response to industrial capitalism's exploitation of workers.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Otto von Bismarck's foreign policy approach of practical, amoral pursuit of national interest through whatever means were effective — diplomacy, war, or deception — without being bound by ideological or moral constraints.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Japan's 1868 revolution that overthrew the Tokugawa shogunate and restored imperial authority under Emperor Meiji — followed by rapid industrialization, westernization, and military modernization.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A series of Ottoman reforms (1839–1876) that reorganized the military, legal system, and bureaucracy along European lines — a response to European military and commercial pressure.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: China's late Qing reform movement (1861–1895) that attempted to modernize the military and industry using Western technology while preserving Confucian social values — ultimately failed to prevent military defeats by France and Japan.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: He wrote 'The Communist Manifesto' (1848) with Engels and 'Das Kapital' (1867), arguing that history is driven by class struggle, that capitalism exploits the proletariat, and that communist revolution was historically inevitable.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: He wrote 'The Wealth of Nations' (1776), arguing that free markets and division of labor produced the most efficient economies — his work became the theoretical foundation for laissez-faire capitalism.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: She wrote 'A Vindication of the Rights of Woman' (1792), arguing that women's apparent intellectual inferiority resulted from lack of education rather than nature — one of the earliest systematic arguments for women's political rights.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: He led the early Haitian Revolution, commanding formerly enslaved soldiers who defeated French, British, and Spanish armies, inspiring the movement that resulted in the first independent Black republic in the Western Hemisphere — though he was captured in 1802 and died in French prison before Haiti declared independence on January 1, 1804.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: He led multiple South American independence movements (Venezuela, Colombia, Peru, Bolivia) and articulated the 'Jamaica Letter' (1815) arguing for Latin American political unity and independence from Spain.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: He unified the German states through 'blood and iron' — three successful wars against Denmark, Austria, and France — using Realpolitik rather than liberal idealism to create the German Empire in 1871.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: This 1791–1804 revolution in the French colony of Saint-Domingue was the only successful large-scale slave rebellion in history — it created Haiti as the first independent Black republic and terrified slaveholders throughout the Americas.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: This 1789 revolution overthrew the French monarchy, declared the Declaration of the Rights of Man and Citizen, passed through a radical phase (Reign of Terror), and ultimately produced Napoleon — its ideas of liberty, equality, and popular sovereignty spread globally.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Japan's response to Western imperial pressure: rapid industrialization, military modernization, adoption of Western legal and administrative institutions, and transformation from feudal to capitalist economy between 1868 and 1910.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The shift from agrarian to factory production that began in Britain in the 1760s, spreading globally and transforming social hierarchies, creating industrial capitalism, the bourgeoisie and proletariat, and eventually driving global imperialism.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1776 colonial rebellion against Britain established the first modern democratic republic based on Enlightenment principles — its Declaration of Independence became a model cited by later revolutionary movements globally.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The process by which populations moved from rural areas to cities, dramatically increasing urban population — a consequence of industrialization that both created the industrial working class and produced new social problems (disease, poverty, social unrest).

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Why did the Industrial Revolution begin in Britain rather than in France, China, or other large states with greater populations?
cards.answer: Britain had a combination of factors absent elsewhere: accessible coal and iron deposits, waterways for transportation, a legal system protecting private property and investment, agricultural enclosures creating a mobile workforce, and colonial markets providing demand.

choices.text: Britain had a combination of factors absent elsewhere: accessible coal and iron deposits, waterways for transportation, a legal system protecting private property and investment, agricultural enclosures creating a mobile workforce, and colonial markets providing demand.
Britain had the world's largest population, which created demand that could only be met through industrial production.
Britain invented the concept of private property, which no other civilization had previously understood.
China and France deliberately chose not to industrialize because their governments preferred agricultural economies.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Which of the following best explains why the American and Haitian revolutions, though both inspired by Enlightenment ideals, produced such different societies?
cards.answer: The American Revolution was led by colonists who were already the privileged elite and did not extend natural rights to enslaved people; the Haitian Revolution was led by enslaved people who took Enlightenment rights rhetoric at its logical conclusion, abolishing slavery and racial hierarchy entirely.

choices.text: The American Revolution was led by colonists who were already the privileged elite and did not extend natural rights to enslaved people; the Haitian Revolution was led by enslaved people who took Enlightenment rights rhetoric at its logical conclusion, abolishing slavery and racial hierarchy entirely.
The Haitian Revolution succeeded because it had more military support from European powers than the American Revolution received.
The American Revolution failed to establish a republic while Haiti successfully created the world's first democracy.
Enlightenment ideas only reached Haiti after the American Revolution had already succeeded, giving Haitian revolutionaries a completed model to copy.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: How did labor unions represent a response to the social conditions created by industrial capitalism?
cards.answer: Industrial capitalism concentrated workers in factories under dangerous conditions with long hours and low wages, while employers had all the bargaining power over individual workers. Collective organization gave workers countervailing power to negotiate better conditions through the threat of collective action (strikes).

choices.text: Industrial capitalism concentrated workers in factories under dangerous conditions with long hours and low wages, while employers had all the bargaining power over individual workers. Collective organization gave workers countervailing power to negotiate better conditions through the threat of collective action (strikes).
Labor unions were formed by factory owners to train workers more efficiently and increase industrial productivity.
Industrial capitalism gave all workers high wages and good conditions, so labor unions formed to maintain these benefits against government attempts to reduce them.
Labor unions emerged because socialist governments required them as an administrative tool for managing the industrial workforce.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: What distinguished Meiji Japan's response to Western imperialism from the responses of China's Self-Strengthening Movement and the Ottoman Tanzimat Reforms?
cards.answer: Meiji Japan undertook comprehensive structural transformation — replacing feudalism with capitalism, the samurai class with a conscript army, the shogunate with a constitutional monarchy — while China and the Ottomans attempted selective modernization that preserved traditional power structures, producing more limited results.

choices.text: Meiji Japan undertook comprehensive structural transformation — replacing feudalism with capitalism, the samurai class with a conscript army, the shogunate with a constitutional monarchy — while China and the Ottomans attempted selective modernization that preserved traditional power structures, producing more limited results.
Meiji Japan received British military assistance while China and the Ottomans received no foreign aid in their modernization efforts.
Japan was geographically smaller than China or the Ottoman Empire, making modernization easier simply because less territory needed to be reformed.
China and the Ottomans had no interest in modernization and made no efforts to reform their economies or militaries.

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Which of the following best explains how the French Revolution's ideas spread beyond France to inspire revolutions in the Caribbean, Latin America, and later in Europe?
cards.answer: The French Revolution produced universalistic ideological claims (natural rights, popular sovereignty, liberty) that could be applied by any oppressed group, combined with the destabilization of empires during the Napoleonic Wars that created political openings for revolution.

choices.text: The French Revolution produced universalistic ideological claims (natural rights, popular sovereignty, liberty) that could be applied by any oppressed group, combined with the destabilization of empires during the Napoleonic Wars that created political openings for revolution.
France militarily occupied Latin America and imposed revolutionary government on Spanish colonies through direct conquest.
The French government exported revolutionary ideas by funding revolutionary movements throughout the Americas and Europe.
Revolutionary ideas spread only through printed texts, which were exclusively distributed by French government agents to sympathizers abroad.
